behaviors.description: Validate required patient fields and block downstream processing with a complete missing-fields error.
behaviors.justification: This behavior combines field validation correctness with orchestration gating (ensuring downstream modules are not invoked), which is best caught with wired modules/fakes in one process.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake with missing `name` and `insurance_id` using real orchestrator wiring and in-process fakes for insurance/triage/scheduling/confirmation; assert error lists both missing fields and assert no downstream fake was called.

behaviors.description: Reject invalid date of birth values (future, too old, non-date string).
behaviors.justification: DOB validation is deterministic input validation logic at a pure function boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call DOB validator with a future date, a date older than 150 years, and a non-date string; assert each is rejected with the expected validation error.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility request/response contract with payer API is correct.
behaviors.justification: To catch real contract failures, the test should use real HTTP I/O against a controllable API endpoint and verify both outbound payload and status interpretation.
behaviors.test_description: Start a local HTTP test server as payer API, submit intake insurance step, assert request contains correct patient ID and plan code, then return `eligible`, `not eligible`, and `pending` responses and assert mapped internal states.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, mark eligibility pending and continue intake.
behaviors.justification: Unreachable API handling depends on real network/HTTP failure modes (timeout/connection refusal) and continuation behavior under those failures.
behaviors.test_description: Point insurance client to a non-listening endpoint (or force timeout) during intake; assert eligibility is set to `pending` and pipeline proceeds to triage/scheduling instead of aborting.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily wiring/composition between intake data, config, and model client payload formation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute triage call path with configured rubric and known symptom list using a fake model client that captures inputs; assert payload contains both symptom text and rubric content.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage marks emergency symptom combinations as high urgency.
behaviors.justification: The behavior depends on actual model reasoning/output characteristics and non-determinism, not just deterministic parsing or rules.
behaviors.test_description: Send real model triage requests with emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath) across repeated runs and assert urgency classification is consistently high above acceptance threshold.

behaviors.description: Triage parser persists clinician-readable explanation, not only numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Requires parser + persistence wiring to verify explanation field survives mapping into storage schema.
behaviors.test_description: Feed triage parser a model response containing score and explanation, persist via real repository/database adapter, then read back record and assert non-empty explanation field is stored.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation is case-specific and includes symptom-based rationale.
behaviors.justification: Quality/specificity of explanation text depends on real model generation behavior, not deterministic code paths alone.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on multiple distinct symptom sets and evaluate explanations for mention of case symptoms plus urgency rationale; fail if responses are generic boilerplate without case grounding.

behaviors.description: Scheduler chooses first available slot matching urgency SLA (same-day high, <=48h medium).
behaviors.justification: Needs real scheduling logic against shared calendar storage to verify slot selection and SLA window enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Seed calendar DB with representative availability, run scheduling for high and medium urgency cases, and assert selected slot is earliest valid one within required time window.

behaviors.description: Scheduler prevents double-booking under concurrent patient processing.
behaviors.justification: Race-condition detection needs concurrent execution against real shared calendar DB constraints/transactions; no external API required.
behaviors.test_description: Launch two concurrent scheduling requests targeting the same candidate slot against real calendar DB and assert only one booking succeeds while the other is re-routed or rejected without duplicate reservation.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message contains required fields and visit-type-specific prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: Message composition is deterministic formatting/content logic that can be verified at function boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation builder with known patient, appointment, provider, and visit type inputs; assert output includes name, time, provider name, and matching prep instructions.

behaviors.description: Deterministic pipeline orchestration completes correctly with stubbed triage.
behaviors.justification: This validates multi-module sequencing and state handoff with deterministic stubs, without requiring live external services or live model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake in test environment with stubbed triage output and stubbed payer response, real orchestration and storage modules enabled; assert ordered completion of validate->insurance->triage->schedule->confirm and expected persisted artifacts.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes automatically for a real submission.
behaviors.justification: This is a full sequential pipeline with live external payer API plus live agentic triage step; true failure detection requires end-to-end workflow execution.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real intake request in production-like environment with live payer API and live triage model, then verify automatic completion through confirmation delivery and persisted records for each stage without manual intervention.
